★ Avila Pathways · Low-Residency & Transfer Programs

Your route into Avila University.

Low-residency and SEVIS transfers are complex, deadline-driven, and easy to get wrong at the last minute. Avila Pathways lays out every step, every document, and every date — and lets you check the live status of your own case.

Explore the roadmap ★Check my application status
4
Application pathways explained step by step
3
Upcoming intakes with full deadline calendars
8
U.S. residency sites incl. Kansas City
10
Low-res master's programs
The Process, End to End

A clear road map — every step, who does it, and what you need.

Pick your pathway. Tap any step to see exactly what happens, what's required, and — for students — what it means for you in plain English.

Watch: a 2-minute overview
How Avila Pathways works, start to finish.
The journey for a new student joining a low-residency master's or undergraduate program. Blended delivery, two in-person residency days per course, full-time = 6 credit hours per semester. Master's tuition is $19,500 (MBA, 30 credits) or $23,400 (M.S., 36 credits / M.A., 30 credits); undergraduate tuition is detailed in your offer letter. The I-20 is issued for up to 36 months. Minimum GPA 2.5 (Psychology 3.0), with scholarships up to $5,000 for eligible students. Tap any step for the full document list, requirements, and forms.
1
1. Submission, screening & confirmation
Submit the complete file on the GUS portal — Avila then screens every document and issues your Confirmation of Acceptance.
You / Student
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youThis is your starting line. Submit the full document set below through the GUS portal. Financial documents are the ONLY thing you can send later — everything else should be in at submission. As soon as your documents are in, the team verifies them: Avila uses fraudulent-document detection to screen all submissions for authenticity, including bank letters and financial statements. Once your documents are received and reviewed, you receive a Confirmation of Acceptance — written proof your file is live, showing your current status and program details (program name, intake, duration). The more complete your file, the faster everything moves. Get it in before your intake's Complete Application Deadline, and keep your Confirmation and Student ID safe.
⚠
All documents — including bank letters and financial statements — are screened with fraudulent-document detection. Submitting altered, fake, or 'edited' documents leads to immediate rejection, may be reported, and can carry serious immigration consequences. Only ever submit genuine, unaltered originals.
▶ Watch: 1. Submission, screening & confirmation
What happens at this step
  • Submitted via the GUS portal by the student or partner agent.
  • Provide the complete set — application form, passport, transcripts, degree certificate, and proof of English are all needed up front.
  • English test waivers may apply: if you're already in the U.S. and have completed one semester, or via Medium of Instruction (70%+ in English in 12th grade, qualification under 5 years old) — see Admission requirements below.
  • Financial documents are the only items that may follow at a later stage (see the financial step); everything else should be complete at submission.
  • Every document is checked for completeness AND authenticity using fraudulent-document detection software, including bank letters and financial statements.
  • Tampered or fraudulent documents lead to immediate rejection and may be reported.
  • Clean, complete files move forward; anything missing (besides financials) goes On Hold and delays your start.
  • Once received and reviewed, a Confirmation of Acceptance is issued — confirming your documents are in, stating your latest status, and listing your program details (name, intake term, and duration), issued once academic eligibility (GPA / U.S. equivalency) is confirmed.
🎓 Admission requirements
Required documents
  • Completed application form
  • Valid passport
  • Academic transcripts (Bachelor's degree)
  • Proof of English proficiency
  • Degree / graduation certificate
  • Financial documents (may follow at the financial step)
Entry criteria
  • Completed Bachelor's degree (transcript required)
  • Minimum GPA 2.5 (Psychology M.S. requires 3.0)
  • Meet the English proficiency bar (any one test below)
  • Programs are 30–36 credits, 36-month I-20 duration
  • Scholarship of up to $5,000 available for eligible students
  • Initial deposit: $7,500
Accepted English proficiency (any one)
IELTS 6.0Duolingo 95TOEFL 73PTE 53GMAT — Verbal 40+ / Analytical 5.0+GRE — Verbal 158+ / Analytical 4.5+
English test waivers (if you qualify)
  • Already in the U.S.: the English language test can be waived if the student is currently in the U.S. and has completed at least one (1) semester of study there.
  • Medium of Instruction (MOI): accepted if the student scored 70% or above in English in 12th grade, provided that qualification is not older than 5 years.
Documents needed 5 required · 1 optional
✓ Completed application form✓ Valid passport (bio page)✓ Academic transcripts (Bachelor's)✓ Proof of English (see below)✓ Degree / graduation certificateFinancial documents (optional)

★
2. Acceptance Letter issued (within 48 hours)
If no documents are missing (financials excepted), your acceptance letter follows in up to 48 hours.
Admissions
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youIf your file has no missing documents — financial documents are the only exception, since they can come at this later stage — your official Acceptance Letter is issued within up to 48 hours. This is your conditional acceptance, confirming Avila wants you. From here, the financial step opens up.
▶ Watch: 2. Acceptance Letter issued (within 48 hours)
What happens at this step
  • Issued when no documents are outstanding, EXCEPT financials which may still be submitted at this stage.
  • Turnaround: up to 48 hours once the file is otherwise complete.
  • Represents conditional acceptance ahead of final enrollment.
  • If items other than financials are missing, the letter is held until they are submitted and validated.
▴ Hide details
3
3. Financial documents submitted
Prove you can fund your studies — this unlocks your I-20.
You / Student
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youNow you show you can fund your studies. Avila requires proof of funds totalling $25,142 USD for one academic year (tuition and fees, housing, meals, health insurance, and other costs, after the eligible scholarship) for graduate programs. Bank documents must be genuine, original, and recent — they are screened for authenticity. This step is what unlocks your I-20, so don't delay it.

Undergraduate students: the financial requirements are different. The total required for the financial support statement is shown on the official Undergraduate Affidavit form below — use that figure (currently $34,351 USD for the 2025–26 year) as the amount you need to prove.

The Affidavit of Support is only required if you are NOT self-funded — if you're funding yourself, you don't need the affidavit, just the bank statement(s) showing the full required amount.
⚠
Funds required: graduate $25,142 USD or undergraduate $34,351 USD for one academic year (after any eligible scholarship). Add $3,000 per dependent. Internet/printout statements, salary slips, tax returns, and non-liquid assets (property, investments) are NOT accepted.
▶ Watch: 3. Financial documents submitted
What happens at this step
  • GRADUATE programs: the total required for the financial support statement is $25,142 USD for one academic year.
  • Graduate breakdown: tuition & fees $12,300, housing $9,000, meals $4,120, health insurance $1,576, other $646 — less a $2,500 scholarship if eligible.
  • UNDERGRADUATE programs: the total required is $34,351 USD for one academic year — see the official Undergraduate Affidavit form below for the full breakdown.
  • Affidavit of Support: required ONLY if you are not self-funded. Self-funded students don't need an affidavit — just the bank statement(s) covering the full year.
  • If you ARE sponsored, each sponsor provides a notarized Affidavit of Support addressed specifically to Avila University, with a matching original bank statement on official letterhead dated within 12 months.
  • Self-funded students submit an original bank statement covering the full year (no transaction histories or printouts).
  • Student loans: an education loan from an approved education lender is also accepted as proof of funds. Submit the official loan sanction or approval letter on the lender's letterhead, clearly stating your name, the amount approved, and the disbursement terms.
  • Add $3,000 per dependent. International graduate students must live on campus for at least one full academic year.
  • When emailing forms to the university address shown on the form, always CC avilaapplicationmanagement@inuniglobal.com so your application team is copied.
  • NOT accepted: salary/income statements, non-liquid assets, life insurance, pension funds, tax returns, internet-banking printouts, photocopies, or faxed copies.
  • Avila cannot offer federal loans or aid to international students — all funding is the student's responsibility.
Documents needed 2 required · 4 optional
✓ Original bank statement on letterhead (dated ≤12 months)✓ Funds for one academic year — graduate $25,142; undergraduate $34,351Notarized Affidavit of Support (only if NOT self-funded) (optional)Education loan sanction letter (if using a student loan) (optional)+ $3,000 per dependent (if applicable) (optional)Sponsor relationship proof / sponsorship letter (if sponsored) (optional)
📎 Forms & document examples
Affidavit of Support — Graduate (proof of funds form)PDF · tap to open / download ↓ Affidavit of Support — Undergraduate (proof of funds form)PDF · tap to open / download ↓

★
4. Enrollment fee paid — $200 USD
Pay the $200 enrollment fee within 7 days of your admit letter to keep it active.
You / Student
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youThe $200 USD enrollment fee confirms your commitment and triggers the immigration steps. International transfers can take several days to clear, so pay as early as you can and submit proof.
⚠
⏳ Deadline: pay the $200 USD enrollment fee within 7 days of your Admit Letter being issued. Miss the deadline and your application is withdrawn — you can then only apply for the next available intake. International transfers take days to clear, so pay early and keep your receipt.
Video explainer
Explainer video coming soon
“4. Enrollment fee paid — $200 USD”
Placeholder · drop video link here
What happens at this step
  • A $200 USD enrollment fee is paid by the student to keep the Admit Letter active.
  • Deadline: the enrollment fee must be paid within 7 days of the Admit Letter being issued.
  • If the $200 enrollment fee is NOT paid before the deadline, the application is withdrawn.
  • A withdrawn application can only be re-applied for the next available intake.
  • Pay early — bank transfers can take several days to clear, so don't leave it to the last minute.
  • Submit proof of payment so the file can advance to SEVIS.
Documents needed 2 required
✓ $200 USD enrollment fee (within 7 days of admit letter)✓ Proof of payment / transfer receipt
▴ Hide details
5
5. SEVIS transferred
Your immigration record moves to Avila.
Avila University
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youSEVIS is the U.S. government database that tracks your student status. Once your financials and enrollment fee are confirmed, Avila's Designated School Official (DSO) creates or initiates your SEVIS record under Avila so your I-20 can be issued. For transfer students, the timing of the release from your current school's SEVIS matters — coordinate it carefully with that school. It generally takes an average of 3 days for your SEVIS record to be transferred to Avila.
▶ Watch: 5. SEVIS transferred
What happens at this step
  • Initiated once financial documents and the $200 enrollment fee are confirmed.
  • Handled by Avila's Designated School Official (DSO).
  • For new students, a fresh SEVIS record is created under Avila; for transfer students, the SEVIS record is released from the prior school and re-keyed to Avila.
  • It generally takes an average of 3 days for your SEVIS record to be transferred to Avila.
  • For transfer cases, the SEVIS release date must align with the last date of attendance at the prior school.
▴ Hide details
★
6. I-20 issued by Avila
Your I-20 is issued — now pay your tuition deposit within 7 days.
Avila University
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youThe I-20 is the document that proves you're an Avila student for visa purposes. Avila issues it once your SEVIS record is in place. You cannot enroll, pay the visa/SEVIS fees abroad, or enter the U.S. without it. Once your I-20 is issued, you have 7 days to pay your tuition deposit to avoid your application being withdrawn. These deadlines are strict because they're tied to the class start dates — missing them can mean losing your place for that intake. The moment you receive your I-20, check every detail — name spelling, date of birth, program, and dates — and report any error immediately.
⚠
⏳ Once your I-20 is issued, you have 7 days to pay your tuition deposit — miss it and your application can be withdrawn. These deadlines are tied to class start dates, so don't delay. Also check every detail on your I-20 (name, date of birth, program, dates) and report any mistake to your DSO at once.
▶ Watch: 6. I-20 issued by Avila
What happens at this step
  • Issued by Avila once the SEVIS record is in place.
  • Tuition deadline: once the I-20 is issued, you have 7 days to pay your tuition deposit to avoid withdrawal.
  • These deadlines are important because they're tied to the class start dates — a missed deadline can cost you your place for that intake.
  • What the I-20 is needed for: (a) online enrollment at Avila — credentials and class registration are released once it's issued; (b) the F-1 visa appointment at the U.S. embassy or consulate in your home country, where you'll be asked to present the I-20 alongside your passport, DS-160 confirmation, SEVIS fee receipt, and supporting documents; (c) U.S. port-of-entry inspection, where CBP officers verify the I-20 against your visa before admitting you in F-1 status.
  • Pay the SEVIS I-901 fee (currently $350 for F-1 applicants) before your visa appointment — your I-20 carries the SEVIS ID you'll need to do this.
  • Submit your TB test results as part of completing enrollment.
  • Verify all personal and program details on receipt; report errors at once.
▴ Hide details
7
7. Initial program deposit paid
Initial program deposit of $7,500 per university requirements.
You / Student
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youThe initial program deposit of $7,500 USD is paid to confirm your enrollment intent. This is separate from the $200 enrollment fee paid earlier and from the tuition deposit tied to your I-20. The amount is $7,500 USD for both undergraduate and graduate applicants. Submit proof and wait for the university's confirmation.
▶ Watch: 7. Initial program deposit paid
What happens at this step
  • Initial program deposit of $7,500 USD paid per university requirements — applies to both undergraduate and graduate applicants.
  • Separate from the $200 enrollment fee (step 4) and the tuition deposit tied to your I-20 (step 6).
  • Submit proof of payment.
  • University confirms receipt before your credentials are issued for online enrolment.
Documents needed 2 required
✓ Initial program deposit — $7,500 USD (UG and Grad)✓ Proof of payment

Choose where you attend your in-person residency.
You / Student
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youYou pick which approved U.S. site you'll attend for your in-person residency days. New first-year F-1 students attend Kansas City; transfer students may pick their preferred site from the start. Availability can vary by intake — check the Deadlines & Sites table.

★
10. Enrolled
Congratulations — you've made it! 🎓
Avila University
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youCongratulations — you've made it! You are now officially enrolled at Avila University. From here, please work with the communication channels shared with you during your orientation for anything you need going forward. We wish you the very best of luck on your academic journey ahead. Welcome to Avila!
What happens at this step
  • You're officially enrolled and cleared to begin classes on your program start date.
  • From now on, use the communication channels shared with you during orientation for support and updates.
  • Maintain full-time enrollment (6 graduate credit hours/semester) to keep your F-1 status.
  • Best of luck on your academic journey — welcome to the Avila community!
▴ Hide details
For students who have secured a U.S. visa but haven't yet travelled. You're still in your home country. Your SEVIS record must stay in good standing, and you enter the U.S. on your current I-20 before the transfer completes.
1
Application submission
Submit via the GUS portal.
You / Student
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youYour agent or you submit the file. Because you're transferring, you'll include your immigration documents alongside the academic ones. You're still in your home country at this stage, so there's no employment to pause — your work options (including CPT) are handled later, once you've transferred to Avila and your work eligibility has been evaluated.

Financial document submission
Bank docs + affidavit + I-20 transfer form.
You / Student
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youSubmit your financial proof totalling $25,142 USD for one academic year using the official affidavit form, plus the I-20 Transfer Eligibility Form your current school's DSO completes. Pay the transfer fee if one applies.

📧 When you email any of these forms to the university address shown on the form, always CC avilaapplicationmanagement@inuniglobal.com so your application team is kept in the loop.
Video explainer
Explainer video coming soon
“Financial document submission”
Placeholder · drop video link here
What happens at this step
  • Submit the affidavit of support and a matching original bank statement covering $25,142 for one academic year.
  • Complete the I-20 Transfer Eligibility Form — your current school's DSO releases your SEVIS record to Avila using it.
  • When emailing forms to the university address on the form, always CC avilaapplicationmanagement@inuniglobal.com.
  • Pay the transfer fee if applicable.
  • NOT accepted: salary slips, non-liquid assets, tax returns, internet-banking printouts, photocopies.

10
CPT request & work-status evaluation
Apply for CPT — fill the CPT form and submit the supporting documents.
You / Student
▾ Tap for the full detail & documents
What this means for youIf you intend to work using CPT (Curricular Practical Training), this is where it's handled — after your transfer to Avila, not before. You complete Avila's CPT form and submit the additional supporting documents requested. Avila then evaluates your work eligibility, including how the proposed work relates to your degree program. Approval and timing vary case by case, so apply early and don't begin any work until your CPT is authorized.
⚠
Do not begin working until your CPT is formally authorized. CPT must relate to your degree, and the evaluation timeline varies — apply early and wait for written approval before starting any employment.
Video explainer
Explainer video coming soon
“CPT request & work-status evaluation”
Placeholder · drop video link here
What happens at this step
  • Submit Avila's CPT form together with the additional supporting documents requested.
  • Avila evaluates your work eligibility, including how the role relates to your degree.
  • Processing times vary case by case — apply early.
  • Do not start work until your CPT is formally authorized in writing.
Documents needed 3 required
✓ Completed CPT form✓ Supporting documents requested for CPT✓ Evidence the role relates to your degree program

For students in the U.S. on a non-F-1 visa (H4, L2, B2, F2) changing to F-1 while staying in the country. There is no SEVIS transfer — a brand-new SEVIS record is created and you file Form I-539 with USCIS. You generally cannot start classes until USCIS approves.

What happens at this step

  • When emailing forms to the university address on the form, always CC avilaapplicationmanagement@inuniglobal.com.
  • Pay the transfer fee if applicable.
  • NOT accepted: salary slips, non-liquid assets, tax returns, internet-banking printouts, photocopies.

Avila reviews every COS application and will only issue the I-20 once your COS application is visible in SEVIS.

Don't Get Caught Out

The things that go wrong at the last minute.

These are the issues that derail transfers and low-res starts. Read them before you submit.

⚠ OPT ends the day SEVIS transfers

If you're on OPT, work authorization ends automatically on the SEVIS transfer release date — even if your EAD card shows a later date. Stop working on or before that day.

⚠ Unused OPT time is gone for good

Any remaining OPT is permanently forfeited on transfer. It can't be paused, split, or reclaimed at the new school.

⚠ COS students can't get CPT from Day 1

With a pending Change of Status, you're not eligible for CPT. Only once USCIS approves and you officially hold F-1 status does CPT become available.

⚠ You start the NEXT available term

On-shore transfers must begin the next available semester. If SEVIS transfers before summer starts, you start in summer — not the term you planned.

⚠ The 5-month rule

Your Avila program must start within 5 months of your last date of attendance (or the transfer release date) at your previous school.

⚠ Can't transfer into a 50%-similar program

On-shore transfers can't move to the same concentration, or any program where more than 50% of modules overlap with your current one.

⚠ No classes until COS is approved

COS students generally cannot begin classes until USCIS approves — unless the school specifically permits attendance while pending. Plan your start date accordingly.

⚠ CPT renews every 8 weeks, not per semester

CPT is tied to the internship course, which runs on an 8-week cycle. Authorization is reprocessed every 8 weeks, not once a semester.

2026 Intake Calendar & Residency Sites

One date to protect: your Complete Application Deadline.

This is the date your full application (all documents except financials, which can follow) must be in. Miss it and your start slips to the next intake. The table also shows which approved residency locations run each intake.

Intake Program Start Residency Sites for this intake
Summer 2 (PG)Mon, Jul 06, 2026Kansas City only
Fall 1 (UG & PG)Mon, Aug 31, 2026All sites
Fall 2 (PG)Mon, Oct 26, 2026All sites
Program start date Residency sites = approved locations running that intake

Approved instructional sites & venues

Residencies run two full days, 8:00 AM–5:00 PM, with faculty and the DSO present. New F-1 students complete their first year in Kansas City; transfer students may attend their preferred site from the start. Tap any marker on the map for the venue and full address.

8 approved residency sites across 7 states Hover or tap a marker for the venue & address
Low-Residency Master's Programs

10 master's programs across 3 specializations.

All blended, 24-month, based out of Kansas City with in-person residencies at approved U.S. sites. Browse by specialization group below.

Showing 10 programs across 3 specialization groups
International Business Prepares you for administrative responsibilities in the global marketplace. Alongside core business fundamentals, you focus on economics, finance, and marketing from an international perspective. Ideal for those aiming at cross-border roles and multinational strategy.
Business Administration Avila's flagship general MBA, built around a broad business foundation and real-world projects. Small class sizes give you direct access to faculty with successful business backgrounds. A versatile path for professionals advancing into management across any industry.
Marketing Develop the strategy behind product, pricing, promotion, distribution, segmentation, and marketing research — with a strong emphasis on global marketing and ethics. You graduate able to design and lead data-informed marketing campaigns. Completed in 10 accelerated courses.
Management One of the most flexible, versatile paths in the program — personalize it by choosing courses from any concentration area. You build leadership tools in communication, organization, and decision-making. Prepares you for leadership roles in virtually any sector.
Accounting Develop advanced accounting acumen through in-depth study of tax and cost accounting, strategic and financial management, marketing strategy, and fraud examination. Strong preparation for finance leadership and progress toward CPA-track knowledge. IACBE-accredited.
Executive Leadership Build a broad leadership toolkit spanning interpersonal skills, organizational development, conflict management, innovation, and performance management. The program focuses on teaching, coaching, and developing leaders who help any business thrive. Built for emerging and current managers.
Finance Increase your financial intelligence and decision-making with advanced tools in financial management, financial markets and instruments, and international finance. You study how to manage financial risk and value securities. A strong fit for finance professionals and any role making financial decisions.
Strategic Human Resources Focus on workforce development, personnel and labor relations, compensation planning, and organizational risk. The curriculum is aligned with SHRM standards and core competencies, with SHRM certification available. You graduate ready to lead and cultivate an organization's greatest asset — its people.
Project Management Combines core business fundamentals with data-driven project management methodologies, tools, and applications. You're prepared to manage complex projects in technology-driven industries, with coursework that builds toward project management certification. STEM-designated and OPT-eligible.
Healthcare Administration Integrates advanced quantitative analysis, scientific methods, and data-driven decision-making with healthcare management. You develop both the technical STEM skills and the strategic management capabilities to lead in the fast-changing healthcare industry. STEM-designated and OPT-eligible.
Data Science Emphasizes machine learning, big data technologies, and data visualization. You learn to transform complex data into actionable insights and to lead data-driven initiatives across industries. STEM-designated and OPT-eligible.
Engineering Operations Applies data-driven methods and technology management to engineering and operations environments. You build the technical and managerial skills to optimize complex systems and processes in industry. STEM-designated and OPT-eligible.
Cybersecurity Management Blends technology management with the strategy and tools to protect organizations from evolving cyber threats. You develop the capability to lead security initiatives and manage risk in technology-driven environments. STEM-designated and OPT-eligible.
Computer Science Management Pairs applied computing foundations with management skills, preparing you to lead software and technology teams. You bridge the gap between technical execution and business strategy. STEM-designated and OPT-eligible.
Business Analytics Designed for those specializing in analytics and business intelligence. You gain the skills to analyze complex business problems and implement data-driven solutions that drive decisions. STEM-designated and OPT-eligible.
Artificial Intelligence Focused on advanced AI technologies including machine learning, neural networks, and natural language processing. You develop the skills to design and implement AI solutions in real business settings. STEM-designated and OPT-eligible.
Digital Marketing Applies technology management to modern digital marketing — analytics, automation, and data-driven campaign strategy. You learn to plan and measure marketing across digital channels and to lead technology-enabled marketing teams. STEM-designated and OPT-eligible.
Psychology Builds advanced knowledge of human behavior, interpersonal relationships, and organizational dynamics, with concentrations available in mental health and organizational development. Strong preparation for those pursuing doctoral study or non-clinical professional roles in mental health. Flexible, accelerated formats fit working schedules.
Digital Marketing & Social Media Management Develops the strategy and hands-on skills behind modern digital marketing and social media — content, campaigns, audience growth, and analytics. You graduate ready to lead brand and social presence across platforms. Delivered in Avila's flexible, accelerated format.

1 Identity verification

Must match the date of birth on your application.

Unauthorised access or misuse of personal data is prohibited and may be reported. We handle your personal data securely and in line with our data privacy commitments and applicable data protection laws.

🔒 How your data is protected

How this works. Your status is matched from the university's latest applicant data, refreshed whenever a new file is uploaded. Each record shows your intake, program, current status, any missing documents, and your next steps. (Demo: try AV1029867 with date of birth 1999-04-12 .)
Questions, Answered

Frequently asked questions.

Straight answers on residency, CPT, OPT, enrollment, and the things students ask most.

Are residency sessions full-day, and on weekends?+
In-person residency sessions run two full days, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, totalling 18 contact hours. Faculty and the DSO are present throughout. Each 3-credit course is 8 weeks: about 75% of contact hours are in-person, with the rest delivered synchronously online (~3 hours/week).
Do I attend my first residency in Kansas City?+
New international students in F-1 status generally complete their first academic year in person at the Kansas City campus before becoming eligible for the low-residency format. Transfer students may attend their preferred instructional site from the start of the program.
Will every residency be at the same location?+
You may attend any approved residency location based on availability, but which sites run depends on the intake. For example, the Summer 2 intake runs at Kansas City only, while Fall 1 and Fall 2 run at all sites. Check the Residency Sites column in the deadlines table for each intake.
What's the course load and the 24-month plan?+
Programs run on an 8-week course structure over a 24-month plan. Most courses are 3 credit hours, taken one at a time in sequential 8-week sessions. F-1 students maintain full-time status with 6 graduate credit hours per semester — typically two courses each in Fall and Spring.
Can I take a reduced course load or a break?+
You may enrol in just one course in your final semester if approved for a reduced course load under university and federal rules. Breaks can fall between major terms, during university holidays, or in summer terms where enrolment isn't required and you stay F-1 compliant. Continuous academic engagement and satisfactory progress are required throughout.
My I-20 is 24–36 months but the program is shorter. Can I use the full time?+
Yes. Students may use the full 24–36 month (up to 3-year) duration shown on the I-20, depending on their program, enrolment pattern, and credit-hour requirements.
Can low-res students take a summer break and keep CPT?+
Yes — low-residency students may take a summer break and CPT is permitted during it. However, you must remain enrolled in the required internship course during all terms in which CPT authorization is active.
How often is CPT renewed?+
CPT is tied to the required internship course, which runs on an 8-week cycle. So CPT authorization and renewals are processed every 8 weeks — not on a semester basis.
What if I lose my job or change employers during CPT?+
If you lose employment, notify your DSO and academic advisor — you'll be withdrawn from the internship course tied to CPT. If you simply change employers while already CPT-authorized and enrolled in the internship course, that's allowed mid-semester.
What if my employer won't sign the CPT agreement form?+
You're required to submit the CPT agreement form (or an equivalent supporting document) for CPT authorization to be processed.
Can I get CPT on a pending Change of Status?+
No. Students with a pending COS application are not eligible for CPT. Once COS is approved and you officially hold F-1 status, you become CPT-eligible.
What if my COS is still pending after the program starts?+
You remain ineligible for CPT until COS is approved and F-1 status is granted. Schools often defer the program start date if approval is delayed, to keep SEVIS compliant. You generally can't begin classes until COS is approved.
Can a student on OPT apply to transfer?+
Yes, but the SEVIS transfer release date must be planned carefully — once SEVIS transfers, OPT ends automatically. Any remaining OPT time is permanently cancelled and can't be carried forward. You must stop working on or before the release date.
Can I apply for OPT again after transferring?+
Only if you complete a different degree level (e.g. moving up from one level to a higher one gives you another 12 months after one academic year). If you transfer at the same degree level and already used OPT there, you're not eligible for another 12 months. STEM OPT extensions also end on the SEVIS transfer release date.
When is enrolment officially confirmed?+
Enrolment is confirmed once: SEVIS transfer is completed, the Transfer I-20 is issued, required deposits are paid, and the TB testing certificate is submitted.
Is there a payment plan for Summer 2026?+
No — payment plans aren't available for the summer intakes.
A Avila PathwaysPowered by InUni Global

Your route into Avila — low-residency and SEVIS transfer support for international students. This roadmap is a guide; final decisions and timelines rest with the University, USCIS, and your DSO.
